source: P18 standards-alignment paper (informative; https://digitaltrustinfrastructure.org - see publication/HOW-TO-CITE.md)

requirements.text: Trust Network profile MUST NOT be claimed without Core Identity profile for the same operator scope
Federation profile MUST NOT be claimed without Trust Network profile for the same operator scope
Conformance claims MUST list every profile, Extended sub-module, and Reliance Extension sub-module satisfied
An implementation MUST NOT claim a profile unless all profiles in its depends_on chain are also claimed and satisfied
Conformance claims MUST name the ODTIS spec version against which requirements and tests were evaluated
Extended sub-modules MUST NOT weaken Core Identity, Trust Network, or Federation requirements
Implementations MUST NOT use prohibited ODTIS claims including ODTIS certified without statement, Full ODTIS without listing profiles, or eIDAS/QTSP equivalence from ODTIS alone
Conformance statements MUST include odtis_version, profiles, extended_modules, level, operator, scope, requirements reference, tests summary, date, and contact
Implementations MUST NOT imply Trust Network, Federation, Operator, or Extended conformance when only Core Identity is declared
Implementations MUST pass all applicable conformance tests for declared profiles and level, or mark tests partial and list pending test IDs without waiving MUST requirements
A Core Identity implementation MUST support LoA Low, Medium, and High with criteria documented in operator policy
A Core Identity implementation MUST expose the active LoA to RPs in OIDC claims and verification API responses
A Core Identity implementation MUST NOT assign High LoA without successful biometric proofing per operator policy
National LoA MUST be claimed only when Extended sub-module E-Registry is declared and registry adapter rules are satisfied
Operator documentation MUST map VenID LoA levels to NIST IAL/AAL equivalents used for cross-border disclosure
Trust Network implementations MUST document FAL controls for gateway-to-gateway exchange
RPs MUST be able to configure minimum LoA per client application
Verification API MUST return current LoA even when attribute release is denied
Trust Network implementations MUST route all partner exchange traffic through the local exchange gateway
The gateway MUST operate in receiver and sender modes with a unified protocol
Remote nodes MUST be reached via peer gateway base URLs, not exposed backend URLs
Gateway-to-gateway communication MUST use mutually authenticated TLS with partner client certificates
Receiver mode MUST validate partner identity against the trust registry before routing
Implementations SHOULD validate request timestamps and anti-replay identifiers on inbound exchange
Implementations MAY require RSA-SHA256 body signatures per operator policy
Trust Network MUST maintain a catalog of exposed services with stable service identifiers
Access MUST be authorized via service_access_grants binding caller, provider, and service
Static partner permissions, if used, MUST NOT override denied service grants
Grant changes MUST be auditable
Sender mode MUST resolve remote routes from synchronized network configuration
Cache refresh MUST be configurable; stale cache behavior MUST be documented in operator policy
Autodiscovery pattern (`@VenPartnerService`) SHOULD be supported for multi-node service registration
Trust Network MUST operate a documented PKI hierarchy for partner and service certificates
Implementations MUST validate certificate revocation (CRL or OCSP per operator policy) before exchange
Trust Network SHOULD support trusted timestamping for signed exchange messages where policy requires
PKI ceremony documentation MUST be maintained by the DTI operator
Exchange events MUST be logged with correlation IDs suitable for regulator export
DTI operator MUST publish SLA targets for gateway availability compatible with Core Identity RNF-07 where both profiles are claimed
Trust Network SHOULD align zero trust controls with NIST SP 800-207 per deployment profile
Synchronized service catalog entries for remote peers MUST include stable service_id and peer gateway_base_url suitable for sender route resolution
Sender gateway MUST resolve outbound routes by service_id from synchronized catalog without requiring a single hard-coded remote gateway URL for all peers
Exchange gateway MUST fail closed when service grant validation fails; MUST NOT route on implicit network-zone trust
Trust network metadata stores MUST NOT persist full business payloads as authoritative copies; routing metadata and audit envelopes only (Book 1 D4)
Grant request, approval, and revocation workflows MUST emit auditable events when operator policy uses explicit service access grants
Core Identity MUST expose `/.well-known/openid-configuration` and JWKS for token validation
Core Identity MUST support Authorization Code flow; public clients MUST use PKCE
Core Identity MUST issue JWT `access_token` and `id_token` with configurable expiry and refresh rotation
Core Identity MUST support MFA for sensitive operations and SHOULD support WebAuthn/Passkeys where operator policy enables
Core Identity MUST validate `redirect_uri` against registered RP client configuration
Custom LoA and verification claims MUST be derived from identity-core state, not solely from IdP session
Attribute claims beyond standard OIDC MUST NOT be released without recorded consent
Core Identity MUST terminate IdP session on logout and SHOULD support OIDC RP-Initiated Logout with refresh token invalidation
Core Identity MUST offer account recovery flows with identity verification, rate limiting, and auditable security events
Core Identity MUST assign a stable `subject_id` at first successful registration
Core Identity MUST support document-based proofing for Medium LoA
Core Identity MUST support biometric proofing for High LoA with liveness detection
Core Identity MUST support manual review queue for inconclusive automated proofing
Core Identity SHOULD retain biometric raw images only per minimization policy; derived scores or templates per operator policy
Core Identity MUST expose a verification API authenticating RP clients via client credentials or mTLS
Verification API responses MUST include verification status and current LoA
Verification API MUST return only consented attributes within contracted scopes
Verification API SHOULD meet P95 latency targets compatible with real-time flows (design target ≤2s, RNF-11)
Core Identity MUST allow operator registration of RP clients with redirect URIs, scopes, and policies
Core Identity MUST support activation, deactivation, and credential rotation for RP clients
RP client secrets MUST NOT be stored or transmitted in clear text
Core Identity MUST provide a citizen-facing portal showing verification status and active LoA
Core Identity SHOULD allow citizens to view connected RPs and revoke consents
Citizen-facing flows MUST be available in operator-configured locale(s); responsive web is minimum
All public identity endpoints MUST use TLS 1.2 or higher
Core Identity MUST implement rate limiting on authentication and verification endpoints
Security events (login failure, consent change, verification) MUST be auditable with correlation IDs
Core Identity MUST obtain explicit user consent before first attribute release to each RP
Consent records MUST bind subject, RP client_id, scopes, purpose, and timestamp
Users MUST be able to revoke consent; revocation MUST take effect on subsequent requests
Verification API and IdP MUST NOT return attributes outside active consented scopes
Consent UI MUST display RP name, requested attributes, and purpose in citizen-readable language
Operators MUST publish privacy policy and retention rules aligned with applicable law
Implementations MUST support data-subject access requests through documented operator processes
Personal data MUST be encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and sensitive fields at rest
Identity attributes MUST NOT be sold or repurposed for unrelated profiling
DTI operator MUST define RP admission criteria before production onboarding
RP contracts SHOULD specify post-receipt data minimization and breach notification duties
Operator MUST be able to suspend RP clients for abuse or contract breach
E-Wallet implementations MUST support holder-signed Verifiable Presentations (OID4VP)
E-Wallet MUST validate issuer trust via operator trust registry before accepting credentials
E-Wallet SHOULD support selective disclosure (SD-JWT or equivalent) for privacy-preserving release
E-Wallet MUST NOT replace Core Identity consent rules for OIDC Path A; both paths share LoA state
National LoA via civil registry adapter MUST be offered only when E-Registry Extended sub-module is declared and registry adapter is active
E-Registry MUST NOT replace civil registry legal authority or issue national ID documents
National LoA MUST be assigned only after successful registry adapter verification per operator policy
Registry adapter MUST use agreed identifier hashing; raw registry biometrics MUST NOT be persisted in Core Identity
Registry adapter activation MUST require deployment Phase 3+ and documented bilateral agreement with registry authority
Every registry verification call MUST emit auditable events with correlation ID and match outcome metadata
Assisted registration flows MUST obtain explicit subject or legal-representative consent with full audit trail
Representative or tutor flows MUST verify legal relationship before attribute release on behalf of another subject
Inclusion channels MUST NOT bypass LoA proofing rules defined for online registration
Inclusion flows SHOULD support operator-configured locales, accessibility, and offline capture with deferred sync where policy allows
E-Webhook MUST allow RPs to register callback URLs, subscribed event types, and shared signing secret via authenticated API
Webhook delivery MUST retry with backoff on failure and log delivery outcomes for operator review
Webhook payloads MUST minimize PII; use opaque subject references where operator policy requires
E-Signature MUST bind each signature operation to a verified subject identity and active LoA meeting RP policy
Signature keys MUST be issued under operator PKI or recognized TSP integration documented in CP/CPS
Signature creation and verification events MUST be auditable with correlation to subject_id and RP client_id
E-KYB MUST verify legal entity identity separately from natural-person Core Identity proofing
E-KYB SHOULD link authorized representatives to verified natural-person subjects before B2B attribute release
Federated exchange MUST be bilateral; non-transitivity MUST be preserved
Federated partners MUST authenticate with network-specific certificates and federation policy
Federation activation MUST be explicit per deployment phase documented by the operator
Federation agreements MUST include remote instance identifier, validity window, and pinned remote trust material before outbound federated routing is enabled
Sender gateway MUST reject federated routes when direct bilateral agreement is absent, even if a transitive path exists through a third instance
Operators SHOULD publish federation agreement metadata to regulators via Regulator API export when Federation profile is claimed
Suspended or expired federation agreements MUST disable federated routing on subsequent requests within documented cache refresh bounds
Federated exchange audit events MUST include local trust instance identifier and remote trust instance identifier
Operator MUST publish scope of services (Core, Trust Network, Extended sub-modules claimed)
Operator MUST maintain documented governance: technology, institutional, operations, commercial units
National-scale deployments SHOULD follow delegated-operator or PPP models with audit rights for the state
Operator MUST provide audited subject administration (suspend, force re-verification) via operator API or console
Operator MUST document phase-appropriate PKI and SOC maturity in conformance statement
Operator MUST NOT claim Phase 4 certification targets while operating Phase 1-2 profile only
Operator MUST maintain Certificate Policy and Certification Practice Statement (CP/CPS) for network PKI
Root and issuing key ceremonies MUST be documented with dual control where HSM is used
Operator MUST operate CRL or OCSP publication for partner certificates
PKI backup, restore, and disaster recovery MUST be tested on a documented schedule
Operator MUST publish availability targets for IdP and verification API when Core Identity is claimed (design target ≥99.5%, RNF-07)
Operator MUST define partner onboarding, certification, and pricing transparency rules
Operator SHOULD publish product and business metrics for ecosystem health (registrations, verifications, error rates, RNF-25)
Operator MUST provide regulator API or export path for aggregated audit metrics without unnecessary PII exposure
Operator MUST support incident response procedures with documented escalation (P07, RNF-06)
Liability allocation between operator, RPs, and citizens MUST be documented in operator terms and RP contracts
Trust Network implementations MUST verify partner identity on every exchange request (no implicit network-zone trust)
Internal microservices handling sensitive identity data MUST NOT be exposed to the public internet
Secrets (RP client secrets, PKI keys) MUST be stored in a dedicated secrets manager (e.g., Vault pattern)
Implementations SHOULD progress toward full zero-trust profile per operator phase plan
Core Identity MUST resist common web attacks (injection, XSS, CSRF, broken authentication) per OWASP-informed design
MFA MUST be available for sensitive citizen and operator actions
Proofing pipeline MUST include liveness detection for High LoA biometric verification
Wallet presentations (E-Wallet) MUST require holder signature on Verifiable Presentations
Operator MUST maintain fraud monitoring metrics (impersonation attempts, manual review SLA)
Core Identity MUST emit auditable events for registration, verification, and LoA changes
Consent grant and revoke MUST emit auditable events with subject, client_id, scopes
Trust Network MUST emit auditable exchange events with correlation IDs
Event payloads MUST include trace_id and timestamp in a standard header envelope
Audit storage MUST support regulator export without exposing unnecessary PII in log bodies
Extended E-Webhook sub-module MUST sign outbound RP webhook payloads (HMAC or equivalent)
Conformance statements MUST declare deployment phase, active Extended sub-modules (if any), and active Reliance Extension sub-modules (if any)
Phase 1 implementations MUST NOT claim Extended sub-modules not activated in production
Operator MUST publish conformance statements in both human-readable and machine-readable forms
Auth, consent, and grant denial paths MUST fail closed without partial attribute release or implicit trust fallback
Operator MUST maintain a reference implementation map or equivalent component-to-profile trace for L2 auditors when claiming VenID-class deployments
A reliance decision under any Reliance Extension MUST identify the relying party and its registered role before a trust signal is acted upon.
requirements.trace_informative: DTI feed (Capa A/B thesis) (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Base

requirements.text: Each reliance decision MUST bind a declared purpose to every attribute or verdict consumed; use outside the declared purpose MUST be refused.
requirements.trace_informative: DTI feed (Capa A/B thesis) (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Base

requirements.text: Each reliance decision MUST carry assurance metadata (assurance level and method) sufficient for the relying party risk tier.
requirements.trace_informative: DTI feed (Capa A/B thesis) (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Base

requirements.text: Each reliance decision MUST emit a tamper-evident audit evidence reference that a supervisor can reconstruct after the fact.
requirements.trace_informative: DTI feed (Capa A/B thesis) (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Base

requirements.text: Each Reliance Extension MUST define a recourse path for contested, failed, or wrongful decisions.
requirements.trace_informative: DTI feed (Capa A/B thesis) (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Base

requirements.text: Each Reliance Extension MUST define revocation and step-up triggers where the underlying trust signal can change state.
requirements.trace_informative: DTI feed (Capa A/B thesis) (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Base

requirements.text: A Reliance Extension MUST NOT weaken or relax any Core Identity, Trust Network, Federation, or Operator requirement.
requirements.trace_informative: DTI feed (Capa A/B thesis) (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Base

requirements.text: A conformance statement claiming Reliance Extensions MUST list each active sub-module and its deployment phase.
requirements.trace_informative: DTI feed (Capa A/B thesis) (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Base

requirements.text: An agent reliance decision MUST resolve a verifiable agent identifier bound to a named principal and an accountable human or organizational sponsor.
requirements.trace_informative: agentic-ai-odtis-alignment; estonia-ai-id (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Agent-Authority

requirements.text: Delegated agent authority MUST be expressed as a signed mandate with allowed actions, prohibited actions, and value/time/session limits; each delegation hop MUST narrow scope and MUST NOT escalate privilege.
requirements.trace_informative: agentic-ai-odtis-alignment; estonia-ai-id (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Agent-Authority

requirements.text: A relying party MUST verify agent mandate revocation status within a declared freshness window before honoring a high-risk action, and MUST fail closed when status cannot be confirmed.
requirements.trace_informative: agentic-ai-odtis-alignment; estonia-ai-id (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Agent-Authority

requirements.text: High-risk agent actions such as financial disbursement, identity changes, or regulatory filings SHOULD require an explicit human-anchor reference and step-up in the delegation chain.
requirements.trace_informative: agentic-ai-odtis-alignment; estonia-ai-id (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Agent-Authority

requirements.text: A long-lived credential profile MUST publish its algorithm set, hybrid status, and a documented rotation policy and deprecation trigger.
requirements.trace_informative: digital-id-quantum-resilience (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Crypto-Agility

requirements.text: Relying-party acceptance criteria MUST be declared for classical, hybrid, and post-quantum assertion paths, with audit evidence retained per accepted path.
requirements.trace_informative: digital-id-quantum-resilience (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Crypto-Agility

requirements.text: Issuers SHOULD maintain a cryptographic bill of materials (CBOM) and classify harvest-now-decrypt-later exposure by credential validity and data sensitivity.
requirements.trace_informative: digital-id-quantum-resilience (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Crypto-Agility

requirements.text: An identity lifecycle program MUST record both claimed and observed revocation SLA across every credential system that must fire on offboarding.
requirements.trace_informative: identity-week-fido-hid (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Lifecycle-Revocation

requirements.text: Revocation evidence MUST unify physical and digital credential status into a reconstructable audit reference per lifecycle event.
requirements.trace_informative: identity-week-fido-hid (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Lifecycle-Revocation

requirements.text: Operators SHOULD scan for orphan credentials and active sessions at defined intervals after a subject exit.
requirements.trace_informative: identity-week-fido-hid (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Lifecycle-Revocation

requirements.text: A document verification reliance decision MUST declare capture mode (live SDK, upload, or hybrid) and the injection-detection and document-forensics tiers in scope.
requirements.trace_informative: shufti-deepfake (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Document-Capture

requirements.text: Reusable document verification outcomes MUST carry capture-channel integrity and device/session risk evidence; upload-only paths MUST be policy-gated by risk tier.
requirements.trace_informative: shufti-deepfake (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Document-Capture

requirements.text: Verification providers SHOULD disclose lab-versus-production validation metrics and define a fraud recourse and SAR escalation path.
requirements.trace_informative: shufti-deepfake (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Document-Capture

requirements.text: A liveness reliance decision MUST declare the liveness provider, challenge type, identity-linkage class, and retention policy.
requirements.trace_informative: google-liveness-captcha (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Liveness

requirements.text: A human-presence (liveness) verdict MUST NOT be reused outside its declared purpose, and MUST NOT be treated as identity proof or statutory age proof without explicit authorization.
requirements.trace_informative: google-liveness-captcha (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Liveness

requirements.text: Liveness reliance MUST provide an accessible non-camera fallback of equivalent outcome and a recourse path for false rejection.
requirements.trace_informative: google-liveness-captcha (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Liveness

requirements.text: Audience-bound audit disclosure MUST define per-relying-party-role disclosure sets bound to purpose, with prohibited fields enumerated per tier.
requirements.trace_informative: selective-disclosure-scitt (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Disclosure-Assurance

requirements.text: A verifier MUST be able to reconstruct a disclosed verdict offline from the registered evidence and the authorized disclosure set alone.
requirements.trace_informative: selective-disclosure-scitt (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Disclosure-Assurance

requirements.text: A demand for fields outside the authorized disclosure set MUST be refused and MUST trigger a documented recourse path.
requirements.trace_informative: selective-disclosure-scitt (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Disclosure-Assurance

requirements.text: Adoption of an external credential standard MUST be gated by a declared maturity level; First Public Working Draft components MUST NOT be claimed for production national mandates.
requirements.trace_informative: w3c-vc-lifecycle-barcodes (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-VC-Maturity-Gate

requirements.text: Each gated standard component MUST declare the Capa B controls (relying-party authentication, audit logging, rate limits, version pinning) required before production reliance.
requirements.trace_informative: w3c-vc-lifecycle-barcodes (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-VC-Maturity-Gate

requirements.text: Ecosystems SHOULD run conformance labs and publish internal profiles before promoting a draft standard beyond pilot.
requirements.trace_informative: w3c-vc-lifecycle-barcodes (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-VC-Maturity-Gate

requirements.text: A relying party accepting multiple eIDs MUST publish an acceptance matrix mapping each issuer and assurance level to service tier and purpose.
requirements.trace_informative: sweden-state-eid (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Public-eID

requirements.text: Authentication success MUST be distinguished from authorization or eligibility, and cross-border acceptance MUST declare an assurance mapping.
requirements.trace_informative: sweden-state-eid (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Public-eID

requirements.text: Wrongful-rejection recourse and trust-event logging SHOULD be defined before multi-eID operation at scale.
requirements.trace_informative: sweden-state-eid (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Public-eID

requirements.text: A reusable verification outcome MUST carry portable assurance metadata: proofing level and method, PAD and IAD coverage, and capture-channel integrity.
requirements.trace_informative: ai-kyc-pipeline-integrity (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Assurance-Portability

requirements.text: A relying party MUST evaluate step-up requirements against its own risk tier before reusing assurance, rather than inheriting a prior yes/no decision.
requirements.trace_informative: ai-kyc-pipeline-integrity (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Assurance-Portability

requirements.text: Only assurance that is actually portable SHOULD be reused; non-portable assurance SHOULD trigger re-verification.
requirements.trace_informative: ai-kyc-pipeline-integrity (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Assurance-Portability

requirements.text: A fraud decision (block, step-up, hold, or refund) MUST be reconstructable as a trust-event chain linking KYC evidence, fraud signal, and where applicable the Verification of Payee result.
requirements.trace_informative: finextra-fraud-psd3 (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Fraud-Orchestration

requirements.text: Each fraud decision MUST record a liability owner and the legal basis for any cross-institution fraud-signal sharing.
requirements.trace_informative: finextra-fraud-psd3 (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Fraud-Orchestration

requirements.text: A recourse path SHOULD be defined for wrongful blocks as well as for fraud victims.
requirements.trace_informative: finextra-fraud-psd3 (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Fraud-Orchestration

requirements.text: A CIP reliance decision MUST record the verifying institution, proofing method, assurance level, and reliance type (direct verification versus institutional reliance).
requirements.trace_informative: us-stablecoin-genius (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Stablecoin-CIP

requirements.text: Institutional reliance MUST reference an annual AML/CFT and CIP certification and MUST bind the decision to account-opening purpose.
requirements.trace_informative: us-stablecoin-genius (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Stablecoin-CIP

requirements.text: A protocol-agnostic credential presentation envelope and an identity-error recourse path SHOULD be supported.
requirements.trace_informative: us-stablecoin-genius (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Stablecoin-CIP

requirements.text: A cross-border travel reliance decision MUST declare, per touchpoint, the relying party, purpose code, and allowed attribute set with an assurance-level mapping.
requirements.trace_informative: wttc-biometria-travel (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Travel

requirements.text: Attribute reuse across touchpoints MUST be bounded by a declared reuse policy (one-time, session-bound, or journey-bound).
requirements.trace_informative: wttc-biometria-travel (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Travel

requirements.text: A recourse endpoint and cross-border recognition scope SHOULD be published per touchpoint.
requirements.trace_informative: wttc-biometria-travel (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Travel

requirements.text: A software-supply-chain trust profile MUST attest manufacturer versus steward role and maintain SBOM provenance per release.
requirements.trace_informative: cra-readiness-reality (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-CRA-Resilience

requirements.text: Active-exploit and severe-incident events MUST be recorded as a trust-event chain with corrective-measure and regulator-filing references and timestamps.
requirements.trace_informative: cra-readiness-reality (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-CRA-Resilience

requirements.text: Post-market monitoring, a vulnerability-disclosure policy, and a component revocation path SHOULD be documented.
requirements.trace_informative: cra-readiness-reality (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-CRA-Resilience

requirements.text: A shared-infrastructure (DPI) trust profile MUST publish participant and relying-party authorization scoped per component, with blast-radius mapping of co-dependent services.
requirements.trace_informative: dpi-attack-surface-blast-radius (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-DPI-Resilience

requirements.text: A Trust Resilience Evidence Pack covering authorization, purpose, assurance, audit, revocation/step-up, recourse, and reporting MUST be maintained from pilot day one.
requirements.trace_informative: dpi-attack-surface-blast-radius (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-DPI-Resilience

requirements.text: Security and incident metrics SHOULD be reported transparently to partners, regulators, and the public.
requirements.trace_informative: dpi-attack-surface-blast-radius (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-DPI-Resilience

requirements.text: Reliance on a sovereign or external identity-chain credential MUST document the chain operator accountability and issuer liability and revocation before mutual recognition.
requirements.trace_informative: china-identity-chain (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Sovereign-Chain-Interop

requirements.text: Technical interoperability with an external identity chain MUST NOT be treated as institutional trust transfer; cross-jurisdiction acceptance MUST declare assurance mapping, purpose binding, and a recourse path.
requirements.trace_informative: china-identity-chain (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-Sovereign-Chain-Interop

requirements.text: A law-enforcement biometric reliance decision MUST record purpose, the alert-list authorizer, retention policy, and deployment class (overt or covert).
requirements.trace_informative: wa-police-lfr (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-LE-Biometric

requirements.text: False-match mitigation and a citizen recourse path MUST be defined before operational use.
requirements.trace_informative: wa-police-lfr (manuelmerida.io DTI feed); Capa B; sub-module R-LE-Biometric
